metadata.title:
Pledges Federation Aid in
Combating Federal


Decision.

pages.raw_text:
The Spokane Central Labor
union last night went on record in
unmistakable protest against the
recent court decision sentencing
GOmpers, Morrison and Mitchell to
prison for contempt of court in dis
obeying the Buck stove injunction.
The resolutions adopted unani-
mously are in a measure revolu-
tionary. They follow:


Whereas, History has proven
time and time again that there
come times in the continued pro-
gress Of the human racce to a high
er and better civilization, when 1t
is necessary to break the bonds Of
the existing order of things which
would stay us in political and In
dustrial condition that has not
kept pace with the growing uplift
of humanity. 1t is not necessary
to here recount such crises and
the outcome, but 1t is well remem-
bered that at one time 1t became
necessary for king of England
to have the heads of seven judges
removed in order to give a meas
ure of justice to the people, which
had been narrowed and denied by
the precidents and decisions of
courts; and again, in our own coun-
try. a sacrifice of life and property
was necessary to establish justice
over a court decision for a large
part of the human family;


Whereas, 1t appears from recent
United States court decisions that
another such period of judge-made
injustice is upon us, in that during
the past year both the supreme
court and other United States
courts have rendered decisions
which have shorn labor organiza-
tons of much of their power to aid
In the upliftment of humanity;
